concerns.text: Phase 5b (T031-T036) violates the 'Producer before Consumer' and 'Foundational tasks first' principles by introducing a new research scope (Scaling Analysis) that has no upstream artifact (spec/plan) to consume. The 'Notes' section explicitly states this phase was 'Removed' to resolve scope creep, yet the tasks remain in the execution list. This creates a semantic dependency violation where tasks exist without a defined data-flow source or approved requirement.

T008 (Setup directory structure) and T008a (Checksum utility) create a fragile dependency chain. T009/T010 depend on T008a, but T008 is listed as a separate task that 'does NOT implement the checksum utility'. If a developer executes T008 and assumes data hygiene is partially complete before T008a runs, they may process data without checksums, violating the data-flow requirement that 'Raw data checksummed' (Constitution Principle III) must precede any data transformation.

T006a (Create deviation record) and T006 (Generate transparency report) have a strict ordering dependency. T006 reads the file created by T006a. While T006a is marked non-parallel, T006 is not explicitly marked as dependent in the task header (only in the description). The 'Dependencies & Execution Order' section correctly notes T006a must complete before T006, but the task list itself lacks a clear visual dependency marker (e.g., 'Depends on T006a') in the T006 line, risking parallel execution if a developer relies solely on the [P] tag logic.

The 'Notes' section claims 'Phase 6 (Scaling Analysis) has been removed' but the file contains 'Phase 5b' with the exact same tasks. This is a documentation contradiction that obscures the true execution order and scope, making it difficult to verify if the 'Removed' note applies to the visible tasks or a phantom phase.
